discussion_comments.comment_body: I get what you are doing with this, but would you be able to get the step order from the `<block>.json` file or is that not possible?
@majestic-owl448 Good point  I'll refactor `isLastStep` to read the step order from `<block>.json` instead of scanning frontmatter. That's cleaner and more reliable.
doesn't this already count the editable region markers?

@majestic-owl448 `findRegionMarkers` counts markers per individual seed file and throws if a single file has more than 2. The workshop-level check in `add-seed.js` counts across all seed files in the challenge to ensure exactly 2 total. They serve different purposes — happy to add a comment clarifying this if useful.
alright, yes, counting per individual seed file is what we want, isn't it redundant to have both then?

@majestic-owl448 Actually they serve distinct purposes — `findRegionMarkers` validates that no single seed file has more than 2 markers, but it doesn't enforce that exactly 2 markers exist across the entire workshop. A workshop could have multiple seed files each with 0 markers and pass `findRegionMarkers`, but the workshop-level check ensures exactly one editable region exists in total. Removing it would allow workshops with 0 or 4+ total markers to slip through. Happy to add an inline comment to make this distinction clearer.
so isn't `findRegionMarkers` redundant now? if it fails the new check is also failing, with the new check failing on more things

@majestic-owl448 Good point — I've added inline comments to clarify that the two checks serve distinct purposes. findRegionMarkers catches per-file violations (a single file with 3+ markers), while the workshop-level check enforces exactly 2 total across all seed files combined. The comment makes this distinction explicit in the code now.
